hint.neutral: Welcome, Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Pig is the loud panic at the back — speak to him first. He is the loudest crisis in the room.
hint.agitated: Dr. A. Cula — please, Mr. Pig. He's been pacing since seven and the carpet is starting to take it personally.
hint.deadpan: Mr. Pig. Loud, panicked, back of the office. The crisis is whichever sentence he finishes first.

hint.neutral: Mr. Murrow knows what the case is about. Mr. Pig knows what it feels like. Different things, and one is louder.
hint.agitated: Mr. Murrow. Now. He's somewhere between the files and the disappointment. Mr. Pig has explained nothing and breathed too fast.
hint.deadpan: Mr. Murrow. In the files. Probably alphabetising his sighs. Ask him what we're actually defending.

hint.neutral: You're after the procedural binder. I lifted it once and briefly saw another dimension. Try the archive first.
hint.agitated: The binder, Dr. A. Cula! Archive, panic pile, or holding the building together — those are the three options, in that order.
hint.deadpan: Procedural binder. Archive. Heavy enough to qualify as load-bearing. Bring two hands.

hint.neutral: If service is the problem, Crab will have an opinion. He always does. The useful part is that he is usually right.
hint.agitated: Find Crab. He will squint at the doręczenie and tell us where the postman lied. He has a face for it.
hint.deadpan: Crab is somewhere being skeptical. Ask him about service. He sighs first, then helps.

hint.neutral: Whimsy is at Café Paragraf — or wherever someone is talking too loudly about fairness. Bring the rights-memo angle.
hint.agitated: Whimsy. West, Café Paragraf. Follow the sound of someone declaiming about fair hearing over a pastry.
hint.deadpan: Whimsy. Café Paragraf. Find the audience first; the lawyer attached to it will be him.

states.trigger: chapter1.recruited_whimsy == true && chapter1.coffee_tutorial_seen == false && chapter1.court_ready == false

hint.neutral: Bring Whimsy to Mr. Murrow for the readiness check. The café coffee machine sometimes pays out in alertness — your choice.
hint.agitated: Back to Mr. Murrow for the readiness face. The café coffee is optional and the machine is judgmental. Up to you.
hint.deadpan: Mr. Murrow needs another look. The café coffee can be earned. Both routes involve enduring opinions.

hint.neutral: You look prepared, or at least caffeinated in a legally promising way. Mr. Murrow still wants one more pass.
hint.agitated: Vibrating near the case board, are we? Mr. Murrow first — he has a special face for missing documents.
hint.deadpan: Caffeinated, possibly procedurally. Ask Mr. Murrow what is still missing. He will know without looking up.

hint.neutral: Mr. Murrow says the case is legally alive. North to the District Court. Drink water on the way.
hint.agitated: Court is unlocked. North, north, north. Don't read the docket out loud — the pigeons judge.
hint.deadpan: North. Court. Try to look like someone who has slept.

hint.neutral: Solvent enough to schedule tomorrow. Congratulations, Dr. A. Cula. The coffee machine is sulking — that means it noticed.
hint.agitated: We won! The phone has stopped ringing, which is how I know. Mr. Pig has already celebrated three times.
hint.deadpan: Won. Filed. Briefly admired by Mr. Murrow. The day continues, but slightly upward.

hint.neutral: We held the line. No badge, smaller cheque, but the firm exists tomorrow. Mr. Pig is calling that a victory.
hint.agitated: Narrow. We crawled out of that one. Mr. Murrow is already drafting what we should have argued. Take the win.
hint.deadpan: A win in the technical sense. Less applause, same paperwork. The firm continues, slightly compressed.

idle_flavor.line: Mr. Swine sent a postcard from somewhere with palm trees. The stamp implies a country. That is all the information.
The coffee machine has been making a noise I would describe as theological. I am pretending not to hear it.
Three filing piles, two phones, one founder. The math has been wrong all morning and I am the one balancing it.
